description_long: This house features a striking modernist design with clean, rectilinear forms and a strong emphasis on horizontal lines. The structure is composed of two main volumes, each topped with a flat, dark roof, and separated by a spacious open terrace area. Large glass panels are visible along the facade, suggesting expansive windows that allow for abundant natural light and seamless indoor-outdoor integration. A prominent swimming pool runs parallel to the main building, bordered by a light-colored deck and a privacy wall. The exterior materials appear to be a combination of concrete, glass, and metal, contributing to the minimalist aesthetic. The overall layout is open and linear, with a focus on outdoor living and visual connection to the surrounding environment.
description: A modern, single-story house with flat roofs, large glass windows, and a long swimming pool alongside a minimalist concrete and metal structure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the elongated layout, main living volume, large pool, and terrace area.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the pool's width, the secondary building volume, and the open terrace between structures.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the main building, the pool's end, and the smaller volume with its own roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the pool, the terrace, and the relationship between the two main building volumes.
attribute_entryway_design: Minimalist main entry with large glass doors, no visible steps, and a flat overhang
